slides.label: Overview
slides.title: Emberflow Platform
slides.prose:
Emberflow is a document publishing platform built for developer tools and technical content. It turns markdown into hosted, interactive pages with comments, diagrams, and AI-powered features.

This overview covers the project timeline, budget allocation, and current risk register as of August 2025.

Status: Core platform shipped. Now building interactive visual explainers to replace static diagrams.

slides.label: Timeline
slides.title: Project Phases
slides.prose:
The project is structured in five phases, from initial discovery through public launch. Each phase builds on the previous — no big-bang releases.

Phases 1-2 are complete. Phase 3 (key features) is 65% through, with the CLI, Spaces, and MCP integration shipped. Comments are in progress.

The interactive visuals initiative (Phase 4) is the current spike — exploring whether AI-generated HTML can replace static diagrams at production quality.

slides.label: Budget
slides.title: Cost Breakdown
slides.prose:
Total project budget is $90,000 across five categories. Development is the largest line item at 50% of the budget, covering two engineers over eight months.

Infrastructure costs are tracking under budget thanks to Neon's free tier covering development and staging databases.

Marketing spend is intentionally back-loaded — most of that budget is reserved for the launch campaign in October.

slides.label: Spend
slides.title: Budget Utilization
slides.prose:
Overall spend is at 65% of total allocation ($58,700 of $90,000), which tracks well against the 70% timeline completion.

Design is the most fully utilized category at 80% spent — the brand system, component library, and icon set are largely complete.

The contingency budget remains untouched. If the interactive visuals spike succeeds, we may allocate part of it to additional template development.

slides.label: Risks
slides.title: Risk Register
slides.prose:
Four active risks on the register, one rated high. The AI output consistency risk is the most significant — it directly impacts whether the interactive visuals feature ships.

Mitigation strategy: constrain the AI with strong design system rules and reference templates, validate output across diverse topics before committing to the feature.

All medium and low risks have known mitigations and are being actively managed.
slides.viz:
AI output consistency
Generated visuals may vary in quality across topics. Mitigate with strong prompt constraints and reference templates.
high risk
Scope creep on visuals
Interactive explainer feature is ambitious. Timeboxed to 6-week spike with go/no-go checkpoint.
medium risk
Auth token expiry edge cases
CLI device flow tokens can expire mid-publish. Needs graceful re-auth without losing content.
medium risk
CDN cache invalidation
Published document updates may serve stale content. Current TTL is 5 minutes.
low risk
